hooks.prompt: First, check if any per-workflow directory matching `ai/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<work-item-id>/tracker.md` exists. Use `find ai -maxdepth 2 -name tracker.md 2>/dev/null`. If none exist, there is no active workflow — skip this entirely and do nothing. (Note: if `ai/plans/` or `ai/tasks/` directories contain .md files, the workspace is on the v1.x layout and `/dev-workflow migrate` must run before any phase resumption — surface that prompt instead.) Otherwise: context was just compacted — critical workflow state may have been lost. Reconstruct it now. Find the most recently-modified `ai/<date>-<id>/tracker.md`. Read it fully. Extract: (1) Story ID from the directory name suffix, (2) every task row with ID, repo if multi-repo, description, and status, (3) current phase — if any task is In Progress or In Review: Phase 3 (TDD Development Loop); all dev tasks Done, human approval not set: Phase 4; human approval set but Test Hardening not complete: Phase 5; Test Hardening complete but security review not stamped: Phase 5.5; security review stamped but no PR: Phase 6; PR exists but not merged: Phase 7 (on-demand) or idle; PR merged but tracker not archived: Phase 8. Check .claude/context/repos-paths.md for repo paths. For each repo get the current branch and run `git -C <repo-path> worktree list`. For every listed worktree whose branch matches `worktree/<story-id>-t<n>-<uid>`, look up task T<n> in the tracker: preserve worktrees whose task is 🔧 In Progress or 🔄 In Review (the resumed session will pick them up); flag worktrees whose task is ✅ Done or has no matching tracker row as candidates for removal (do NOT remove yet — surface the list and ask the human to confirm). Repos running in worktree-failed fallback mode (no worktrees) are valid; do not flag. The plan lives alongside the tracker at `ai/<date>-<id>/plan.md`. Output a structured state block: story ID, tracker path, phase, all task statuses (one line each), worktree reconciliation table (path, branch, task ID, preserve/remove classification), branches, plan path. Then continue the workflow from where it was interrupted.
